query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the summary of findings:

## Answer to the research question

**No — the US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6.**

On **July 1, 2026**, at the mandatory six-year USMCA joint review, the United States declined to confirm the optional **16-year extension under Article 34.7**, stating it "did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form." This is legally distinct from a withdrawal:

- **Article 34.6 (Withdrawal):** 6-month written notice → **NOT invoked**. No withdrawal notice was submitted.
- **Article 34.7 (Joint Review / Extension):** Non-consensus on the 16-year extension → **triggered mandatory annual reviews** running until the Agreement's scheduled expiry on **July 1, 2036**.

**Key context:**
- USMCA **remains fully in force** through 2036; tariffs, rules of origin, and investment protections are unchanged.
- The 16-year extension remains available "at any time" via written confirmation by the three heads of government.
- Canada and Mexico both supported the full extension; only the US declined, so there was no trilateral consensus.
- The US is pursuing **bilateral** negotiations — US–Mexico completed two rounds with a third scheduled for the week of July 20, 2026 in Mexico City; Canada had not yet begun text-based talks.
- Trump's rationale centered on US trade deficits with both partners.

Sources include White & Case, CNBC, NBC News, Foreign Policy, Brownstein (BHFS), CSIS, Mexico News Daily, and the USTR text of Chapter 34, all accessed July 3, 2026. Structured findings have been recorded.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: The US did not provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6; instead it declined to exercise the optional 16-year extension under Article 34.7. Agreement remains fully operational through July 1, 2036. US-Mexico Round 3 scheduled week of July 20, 2026 in Mexico City.

sources.title: U.S. won't renew USMCA, will review trade pact with Canada and Mexico
sources.snippet: Trump administration decided not to renew the trilateral trade pact on July 1, 2026 deadline; USTR hosted a virtual meeting for the joint review.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Opts for Annual Review Process
sources.snippet: Trump administration formally declined to renew USMCA on July 1, triggering an annual review process that extends negotiations.

sources.title: U.S. Decides Not to Renew USMCA, Triggers Annual Reviews
sources.snippet: US declined to confirm the 16-year extension, triggering annual reviews under Article 34.7.4 until the Agreement expires July 1, 2036.

sources.title: Trump won't renew USMCA, toppling one of the last pillars of stability in global trade
sources.snippet: USMCA stays in effect for another decade provided no member withdraws, but triggers yearly reviews. Trump cited trade deficits.

sources.title: USMCA Review 2026

sources.snippet: Article 34.6 allows withdrawal with 6 months written notice; Article 34.7 sets 16-year term with 6-year joint review and annual reviews if not extended.
